name: Forward Curve SCD Router
summary: Slowly-changing-dimension router: given a reference date, split a forward-curve history table into current and historical rows. The canonical cond/~cond shape that consolidates into a single 1-to-2 SPLIT recipe.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Forward-curve history: each row is a (commodity, tenor, delivery_location)
# quote with a validity window. effective_date <= ref_date < end_date means
# the quote is current; everything else is historical.
ref_date = "2026-04-26"
df = pd.read_csv("curve_history.csv")

# Bind the predicate so py-iku consolidates df[cond] / df[~cond] into ONE
# 1-to-2 SPLIT recipe rather than two single-output filters.
cond = (df["effective_date"] <= ref_date) & (
    (df["end_date"].isna()) | (df["end_date"] > ref_date)
)
current = df[cond]
history = df[~cond]

current.to_parquet("curves_current.parquet")
history.to_parquet("curves_history.parquet")

name: Mark Validation vs Broker
summary: Join internal mid marks against an external broker-quote feed on (commodity, tenor, delivery_location), drop nulls, rename to internal_mid / broker_mid, and gate downstream rows for IPV review.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Internal forward curves vs the broker-feed quote tape.
internal = pd.read_csv("curves.csv")
broker = pd.read_csv("broker_marks.csv")

# Inner-join the two snapshots on the canonical curve key.
merged = internal.merge(
    broker,
    on=["commodity", "tenor", "delivery_location"],
    how="inner",
    suffixes=("_int", "_brk"),
)

# Drop rows where either side is missing a mid price.
merged = merged.dropna(subset=["mid_price_int", "mid_price_brk"])

# Rename to the desk-readable column names IPV reviewers expect.
merged = merged.rename(columns={
    "mid_price_int": "internal_mid",
    "mid_price_brk": "broker_mid",
})

# Gate to non-negative internal marks before the IPV review queue.
flagged = merged[merged["internal_mid"] >= 0]
flagged.to_csv("mark_validation.csv", index=False)

name: EOD Curve Blending
summary: Stack two broker curves and the exchange-settle feed, then average mid_price by (commodity, tenor) — the canonical end-of-day blended-curve build.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# EOD curve blend across three sources: two brokers + the exchange settle.
# All three share schema: as_of_date, commodity, tenor, mid_price, source.
broker_a = pd.read_csv("broker_a_curves.csv")
broker_b = pd.read_csv("broker_b_curves.csv")
exchange = pd.read_csv("exchange_curves.csv")

# Stack the three feeds into a candidate tape.
all_sources = pd.concat([broker_a, broker_b, exchange])

# Blended mid: mean of available sources by (commodity, tenor).
blended = all_sources.groupby(["commodity", "tenor"]).agg({
    "mid_price": "mean",
}).reset_index()

blended.to_csv("blended_curve.csv", index=False)

name: Vol-Surface Flatten
summary: Pivot a long-form vol surface (one row per strike) into a wide matrix keyed on (commodity, tenor) — the canonical shape pricing teams ingest into their option-pricing engine.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Listed-options vol surface: rows keyed on (commodity, tenor, strike).
# vol_surface.csv: as_of_date, commodity, tenor, strike, iv, delta.
vol_surface = pd.read_csv("vol_surface.csv")

# Pivot strikes to columns so each (commodity, tenor) is one row of the
# surface matrix — the canonical pricing-team input shape.
flattened = vol_surface.pivot(
    index=["commodity", "tenor"],
    columns="strike",
    values="iv",
)
flattened.to_csv("vol_surface_flat.csv")

name: Forward Curve Build (Last-Tick)
summary: Group intraday tape ticks by tenor, take the last mid_price per tenor, and sort tenor-ordered to produce the canonical last-print forward curve.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Tape ticks throughout the trading day; we build the forward curve from
# the last tick per tenor.
# tape_ticks.csv: timestamp, commodity, tenor, mid_price, bid, ask, source.
ticks = pd.read_csv("tape_ticks.csv")

# Last mid per tenor — the canonical "last-print" forward curve.
last_per_tenor = ticks.groupby("tenor").agg({
    "mid_price": "last",
    "timestamp": "max",
}).reset_index()

# Tenor-ordered for the curve plot.
curve = last_per_tenor.sort_values("tenor")
curve.to_csv("forward_curve.csv", index=False)
